intro: Capitanopsis brevilabra, also known as Cat's Claw, is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as arthritis, ulcers, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Capitanopsis brevilabra include alkaloids, particularly oxindole and indole alkaloids, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
